hypothesis: The interpolated score difference vector used in Score-Aligned Ascent to approximate the reaction coordinate will systematically misrepresent the true reaction coordinate in systems with multiple intermediate metastable states along the transition pathway, and explicitly incorporating intermediate-state conditional scores into a hierarchical score composition scheme will improve transition state identification accuracy—measured by committor value distributions more tightly peaked at 0.5—compared to the binary two-state interpolation used in ASTRA.
